study_metadata.title: Product Validation Interviews — CVBER for Freelance Illustrators
study_metadata.methodology: Remote structured interview, 18 questions, feature scoring

top_missing_features: Social media monitoring beyond art sites

answers.q1_first_reaction: I'd be immediately drawn to the reverse image search. I've found my art on Chinese bootleg sites three times this year. The 12 million sources number is impressive — that's way more than TinEye alone finds. My first thought is 'finally, something comprehensive.' But I'd worry about upload limits and processing time. How long does scanning 12 million sources actually take?
answers.q2_auto_action: I'd want it to automatically draft a DMCA takedown and send it to the hosting provider. Not just alert me — I want the tool to DO the work. I've spent 45 minutes manually filling out DMCA forms. I'd also want it to screenshot the stolen page as evidence before the infringer can take it down.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Absolutely yes. I've had my art used on print-on-demand products and there's zero trace back to me. An invisible watermark that survives screenshots would be game-changing. I'd use it on every single piece. The only concern is file size — I don't want my upload files bloated.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Honestly? Not very important right now. I don't fully understand blockchain beyond the buzzword. If it proves I created something on a specific date, that's useful for legal disputes, but I'd need someone to explain how that actually holds up in court. I wouldn't pay extra for it.
answers.q5_c2pa_certificate: I'm on the fence. I know Adobe is pushing Content Credentials, but my clients don't ask for origin verification. Maybe if I was selling high-end prints or licensing to brands, but for freelance illustration commissions? It feels like overkill. I'd only pay if it directly prevented theft.
answers.q6_whats_missing: The biggest gap is portfolio integration. I upload to ArtStation, Behance, Instagram — and then I'd have to separately upload to CVBER? That's redundant. I want it to automatically monitor wherever I post. Also, there's no client management. I need to track which pieces are licensed to which clients and when licenses expire.
answers.q7_one_feature: Automated licensing tracking. Right now I use a spreadsheet to track who bought what, what usage rights they have, and when payments are due. If CVBER could generate proof of license, track expiration, and alert me when someone uses art beyond their license — that alone would justify a subscription.
answers.q8_daily_usage: If it had a dashboard that showed me real-time alerts when my art is detected somewhere new, I'd check daily. Also, if it integrated with my commission workflow so that when I deliver art to a client, it automatically gets vaulted, watermarked, and tracked — that would make it part of my daily process.
answers.q9_unhandled_protection: Fan art. I do fan art commissions and I want to protect my original interpretations even though the underlying IP belongs to someone else. No tool handles derivative work protection. Also, AI training data — I want to know if my art is being scraped for AI models.
answers.q10_how_often_check: Once a week manually. I do a reverse image search on Google and TinEye for my most popular pieces. But it's tedious and I miss a lot.
answers.q11_platforms_shared: Instagram (main portfolio), ArtStation (professional), DeviantArt (community), Twitter/X (process shots), personal website (commissions page)
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 8-12 finished pieces, plus 20+ sketches and WIPs
answers.q13_current_protection: Watermark visible versions on social media, low-res uploads, reverse image search once a week, keep PSD files locally. That's it. It's not enough.
answers.q14_would_upload_today: Yes, but I'd start with my most popular pieces — maybe 20-30 works. What holds me back is data privacy. Where are my files stored? Who has access? Can they be used to train AI? I need explicit guarantees.
answers.q15_trust_factors: End-to-end encryption, clear privacy policy in plain English, no AI training on my data, SOC 2 compliance, and the ability to delete my data completely. Also, I want to see other artists vouching for it.
answers.q16_ai_analyzing_feeling: Cautiously optimistic. I understand AI is needed to detect theft at scale, but I want transparency about what the AI is looking at. Is it just comparing images, or is it analyzing my style? I don't want my style data used to train generators.
answers.q17_recommend_to_others: I'd say 'It found 12 stolen copies of my art in 5 minutes that I never knew about.' That's the pitch. I'd recommend it based on results, not features. If it actually works, artists will spread the word organically.
answers.q18_must_do: It MUST find stolen copies accurately and generate DMCA takedowns that actually work. If it gives me false positives or the takedowns get ignored, I'm done.

answers.q1_first_reaction: My first reaction is relief. I've been doing editorial illustration for 12 years and theft has gotten worse every year since AI image generation. The combination of vault storage plus search across 12 million sources — that's addressing both storage and detection. I'd want to upload my entire portfolio immediately. My concern is whether it handles high-resolution files — I work at 600dpi for print.
answers.q2_auto_action: I want it to generate a professional DMCA with my legal information, the evidence screenshots, and send it directly to the platform's copyright team. I also want it to track the status — did they remove it? Did the infringer appeal? I need a case management system, not just alerts.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Yes, but I need it to be truly invisible. My clients pay for clean deliverables. If there's any visible artifact, even subtle, that's unacceptable. And it needs to survive not just screenshots but also compression from social media platforms and print reproduction.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Very important for me. In editorial work, timing matters. If a magazine publishes my illustration and someone claims they commissioned it first, I need proof of creation date. Blockchain timestamping is exactly that. I'd use it on every professional piece.
answers.q5_c2pa_certificate: Yes, absolutely. Adobe Content Credentials is becoming industry standard. If a client receives my work with C2PA metadata, they know it's authentic. It adds professionalism and trust. I'd pay a premium for this.
answers.q6_whats_missing: The biggest gap is legal integration. When I find theft, I don't just want a DMCA — I want to assess whether it's worth pursuing legally. Also, there's no international takedown support. Many infringers are overseas and DMCA doesn't apply. I need tools for international copyright enforcement.
answers.q7_one_feature: Client proof generation. When I deliver work, I want to generate a certificate of authenticity with blockchain proof that I can include in the invoice. This protects both me and the client.
answers.q8_daily_usage: If it monitored my existing portfolio across all platforms automatically and sent me a weekly digest of any new detections, I'd use it weekly. Daily would require commission workflow integration — auto-vaulting new deliveries.
answers.q9_unhandled_protection: AI-generated derivatives. Someone can describe my style to an AI and get something similar. No tool currently detects 'this was inspired by / copied from' an artist's style. Also, unauthorized merchandise — my art on mugs, shirts, etc.
answers.q10_how_often_check: Bi-weekly manually. I use Google Reverse Image Search and occasionally TinEye. It's not systematic.
answers.q11_platforms_shared: ArtStation (portfolio), Behance (case studies), LinkedIn (professional), personal website, Instagram (behind-the-scenes)
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 4-6 finished editorial pieces, plus sketches
answers.q13_current_protection: Low-res watermarked versions online, high-res only in client deliveries, manual reverse image search, registered copyrights for major projects
answers.q14_would_upload_today: Yes, immediately. I'd upload my full portfolio. What holds me back is nothing — I've been waiting for a tool like this. My only concern is long-term: will this company still be around in 5 years?
answers.q15_trust_factors: Company track record, legal partnerships, insurance against data loss, clear terms of service, and endorsements from established illustrators I respect.
answers.q16_ai_analyzing_feeling: Comfortable. AI analyzing my art to detect theft is the whole point. I'm more worried about AI being used to CREATE copies of my art than to DETECT copies.
answers.q17_recommend_to_others: I'd say 'This tool found 47 unauthorized uses of my editorial illustrations, including 3 on merchandise sites. The DMCA takedowns were pre-filled and ready to send.' Concrete results sell it.
answers.q18_must_do: Accurate detection with low false positives, and takedown support that actually removes infringing content.

answers.q1_first_reaction: This sounds amazing but I'm skeptical. I've tried Pixsy and they found stuff but couldn't actually take it down. The 12 million sources claim needs proof. I'd want to see a free trial or demo first. My first action would be to upload my most popular character designs — the ones I know are being reposted without credit.
answers.q2_auto_action: I'd want it to send takedown requests but also track the reposts. If someone reposts my art with 'no artist credited,' I want it to contact them and request proper attribution. Not every repost is theft — some people just forget to credit.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Yes but I have concerns about how it interacts with transparent PNGs. Most of my character art is on transparent backgrounds. If the watermark creates artifacts around the edges, that ruins the utility for other artists who want to use my art as reference.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Not important to me. I'm a smaller artist — nobody is disputing my creation dates. I'd rather have the tool focus on finding and removing theft than adding blockchain proofs.
answers.q5_c2pa_certificate: No. My audience doesn't care about origin verification. They care about the art itself. This feels like a feature for bigger artists selling to corporations.
answers.q6_whats_missing: TikTok and YouTube monitoring. My art gets stolen constantly on TikTok — people use my character designs in videos with millions of views. No tool monitors video content. Also, sticker and emoji theft on Discord and Telegram.
answers.q7_one_feature: Social media repost tracking with attribution enforcement. If someone shares my art, I want them to credit me automatically or have the post taken down.
answers.q8_daily_usage: If it had a mobile app with push notifications for new detections, I'd check daily. I'm on my phone constantly. A web dashboard I'd forget about.
answers.q9_unhandled_protection: AI model training. I'm 90% sure my art has been scraped for anime AI models. There's no way to detect this or prevent it currently.
answers.q10_how_often_check: Every 2-3 days. I search my character names and distinctive elements.
answers.q11_platforms_shared: Twitter/X (main), Instagram, TikTok (occasionally), Gumroad (selling assets), Itch.io (free resources)
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 15-20 pieces including sketches, commissions, and personal work
answers.q13_current_protection: Watermark visible versions, low-res uploads, occasional reverse image search. Mostly I rely on my community to report theft.
answers.q14_would_upload_today: Yes, but only 5-10 pieces to test it. I want to see what it finds before committing my full portfolio. Trust is earned, not assumed.
answers.q15_trust_factors: Free tier or trial, artist testimonials, transparent about how AI analyzes images, no hidden terms about data usage
answers.q16_ai_analyzing_feeling: Worried. I've seen how AI image analysis can be used to train generators. If CVBER analyzes my art, what does it learn? Does it create a style profile that could be replicated?
answers.q17_recommend_to_others: I'd say 'Try the free trial — it found 8 stolen reposts of my art in the first hour.' I recommend based on personal results, not marketing.
answers.q18_must_do: Find stolen art accurately and make takedowns easy. Everything else is bonus.

answers.q1_first_reaction: I'd upload immediately. I've had my concept art stolen for game assets — someone literally used my robot designs in their indie game. The vault plus search is exactly what I need. My question is about processing speed — can it handle 4K+ resolution files without throttling?
answers.q2_auto_action: Auto-generate DMCA with evidence package (screenshots, metadata, timeline). Send to platform. Track removal status. Alert me if the infringer reposts elsewhere. I want a complete enforcement pipeline, not just detection.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Yes. I need it to survive game engine compression and texture baking. If my concept art gets turned into 3D assets, the watermark should survive that transformation.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Moderately important. In the game industry, concepts get stolen during development. If I can prove I created a design before a game's release date, that's valuable for disputes.
answers.q5_c2pa_certificate: Yes, for professional work. When I deliver concept art to studios, having C2PA metadata proves it's authentic. It adds credibility to my deliverables.
answers.q6_whats_missing: 3D model theft detection. My 2D concept art gets turned into 3D models and sold on TurboSquid, CGTrader, etc. No tool searches 3D model repositories. Also, texture theft — my textures get repackaged and sold.
answers.q7_one_feature: 3D model similarity detection. If someone converts my 2D concept into a 3D model, I want to know.
answers.q8_daily_usage: If it monitored game asset marketplaces (Unity Asset Store, Unreal Marketplace, TurboSquid) automatically, I'd check daily. These are where my art gets stolen most.
answers.q9_unhandled_protection: AI concept generation. Studios are using AI to generate 'original' concepts that are clearly derivative of existing artists' work. No tool can detect this yet.
answers.q10_how_often_check: Weekly. I search for my distinctive design elements — specific robot designs, color palettes, composition styles.
answers.q11_platforms_shared: ArtStation (portfolio), LinkedIn (professional), Twitter/X (community), personal website, occasionally Reddit
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 6-10 concept sheets, plus individual prop and character designs
answers.q13_current_protection: Watermarked portfolio versions, NDA for client work, manual searches, registered copyrights for major projects
answers.q14_would_upload_today: Yes, full portfolio. What holds me back is file format support — I work with PSD, TIFF, and sometimes EXR. Need to confirm these are supported.
answers.q15_trust_factors: Industry endorsements (other concept artists), technical specifications (encryption standards), file format support, processing speed guarantees
answers.q16_ai_analyzing_feeling: Positive. AI analyzing art to detect theft is a good use of AI. I'm more concerned about AI being used to CREATE art that copies my style.
answers.q17_recommend_to_others: I'd say 'It monitors game asset marketplaces and found stolen derivatives of my concept work.' Game industry artists need this specific functionality.
answers.q18_must_do: Detect theft across game industry platforms (asset stores, game jams, indie dev forums) and generate enforceable takedowns.

answers.q1_first_reaction: I'd be excited but nervous. I illustrate children's books and my work gets stolen for merchandise constantly — t-shirts, mugs, phone cases. The vault feature is great for keeping originals safe. The 12 million sources scan sounds thorough. I'd start by uploading my published book illustrations.
answers.q2_auto_action: I'd want it to send DMCA takedowns to print-on-demand platforms specifically — Redbubble, Teespring, Society6. These are where my art gets stolen most. Also, I'd want it to monitor Amazon for unauthorized products using my illustrations.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Yes, but I need it to work on both digital and printed versions. My art appears in physical books — if someone scans a page, does the watermark survive printing and scanning? That's critical for me.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Important. Children's book illustration is competitive. If someone claims they created a character design before me, blockchain proof would settle it instantly.
answers.q5_c2pa_certificate: Maybe. If publishers started requiring it, I'd adopt it. But currently, no publisher I work with asks for origin verification. I'd need to see industry adoption first.
answers.q6_whats_missing: Print-on-demand platform monitoring is the biggest gap. Redbubble, Etsy (ironic since I sell there), Amazon Merch — these are where theft happens most. Also, AI coloring book theft — my illustrations are being used to create AI-generated coloring books.
answers.q7_one_feature: AI coloring book detection. Someone is taking my illustrations, running them through AI to create coloring book pages, and selling them on Amazon. I need to detect and stop this.
answers.q8_daily_usage: If it monitored Etsy and Amazon specifically for unauthorized products using my art, I'd check daily. Those platforms move fast — stolen products appear and sell thousands before being caught.
answers.q9_unhandled_protection: AI coloring book generation, AI 'style transfer' products, and unauthorized translations of my picture books into other languages.
answers.q10_how_often_check: Twice a week. I search for my character names and distinctive illustration style on Etsy and Amazon.
answers.q11_platforms_shared: Instagram (portfolio), Behance (case studies), Etsy (prints), personal website (commissions)
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 5-8 finished illustrations, plus character designs and storyboards
answers.q13_current_protection: Visible watermarks, low-res uploads, manual searches on Etsy/Amazon, DMCA filings when I find theft
answers.q14_would_upload_today: Yes, but I'd want a children's book specific template — something that understands picture book spreads vs single illustrations.
answers.q15_trust_factors: Children's book industry endorsements, clear explanation of how art is stored, guarantee it won't be used for AI training
answers.q16_ai_analyzing_feeling: Mixed. I appreciate AI catching theft, but I'm worried about AI learning my illustration style and replicating it. I want opt-out options for style analysis.
answers.q17_recommend_to_others: I'd say 'It found 15 unauthorized products using my illustrations on Redbubble and Amazon.' Children's book illustrators deal with this constantly.
answers.q18_must_do: Detect and stop print-on-demand theft on Redbubble, Amazon, and Etsy.

answers.q1_first_reaction: I'd be very interested. I do sports caricatures and they get stolen for merchandise constantly — especially during playoffs and major events. The speed of detection matters — if my art is stolen during the Super Bowl, I need to catch it within hours, not days.
answers.q2_auto_action: I want automated DMCA with priority escalation for time-sensitive theft. Sports merchandise theft is seasonal and fast — I need the tool to move as fast as the infringers.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Absolutely. My caricatures are highly shareable and get reposted everywhere without credit. Invisible watermarking that survives social media compression is essential.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Critical for sports art. There's often a rush to create merchandise around events, and multiple artists might create similar concepts. Proving who created what and when is essential.
answers.q5_c2pa_certificate: Yes, especially for licensing to sports teams and media companies. Origin verification adds value to my licensing deals.
answers.q6_whats_missing: Real-time monitoring during events. When the NBA playoffs start, my art gets stolen within minutes. I need alerts within the first hour, not after a weekly scan.
answers.q7_one_feature: Event-triggered monitoring. When a major sports event is announced, automatically increase monitoring frequency for related art.
answers.q8_daily_usage: During events, I'd check hourly. Between events, weekly. I need flexible monitoring frequency.
answers.q9_unhandled_protection: AI-generated caricatures in my style. Someone can describe 'Marcus Thompson style caricature of LeBron James' to an AI and get something close enough to sell.
answers.q10_how_often_check: During events: daily. Otherwise: monthly. It's very seasonal.
answers.q11_platforms_shared: Instagram (main), Twitter/X (live commentary), YouTube (process videos), personal website
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 10-15 during events, 3-5 off-season
answers.q13_current_protection: Visible watermarks, low-res uploads, manual monitoring during events, occasional DMCA filings
answers.q14_would_upload_today: Yes, but I'd want event-based pricing. I don't need daily monitoring year-round — I need it during playoffs, championships, and major sports events.
answers.q15_trust_factors: Flexible pricing, fast detection speed, proven track record with sports art specifically
answers.q16_ai_analyzing_feeling: Positive. Sports caricature theft is rampant and manual detection is impossible at the speed theft occurs.
answers.q17_recommend_to_others: I'd say 'It caught 23 unauthorized merchandise listings during the NBA playoffs in the first week.' Sports artists need speed.
answers.q18_must_do: Fast detection during events with same-day takedown capability.

answers.q1_first_reaction: I'd be very excited. My anime-style art gets stolen constantly — reposted on websites, used for profile pictures, even sold as prints. The fact that it scans 12 million sources sounds perfect. But I'd worry about language barriers — does it search Japanese and Chinese sites too? Most theft happens on Asian platforms.
answers.q2_auto_action: I'd want it to handle international takedowns automatically. Theft happens on Pixiv, Weibo, Bilibili, and Chinese sites that don't respond to English DMCA. I need multilingual enforcement.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Yes! My art is used as phone wallpapers, Discord profile pictures, and social media headers constantly. Invisible watermarking that survives all those uses would be amazing.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Not very important. In the anime community, style theft is more common than plagiarism of specific works. Blockchain doesn't help with style copying.
answers.q5_c2pa_certificate: No. My community doesn't care about metadata. They care about the art.
answers.q6_whats_missing: Asian platform coverage. Pixiv, Weibo, Bilibili, Naver — these are where anime art gets stolen most. Western tools don't search these platforms effectively. Also, sticker theft on LINE and WhatsApp.
answers.q7_one_feature: Chinese and Japanese platform monitoring. If it could scan Pixiv, Weibo, and Bilibili for unauthorized reposts, that would be revolutionary.
answers.q8_daily_usage: If it had a mobile app and sent notifications when new theft was detected, I'd check daily. I'm always on my phone.
answers.q9_unhandled_protection: AI anime generation. Stable Diffusion models are trained on millions of anime illustrations including mine. There's no way to detect AI-generated derivatives of my specific style.
answers.q10_how_often_check: Every day. I search my art on Google, Pixiv, and social media daily.
answers.q11_platforms_shared: Twitter/X (main), Pixiv (Japanese community), Instagram, TikTok, Gumroad (selling assets)
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 20-30 pieces including commissions, personal work, and fan art
answers.q13_current_protection: Low-res uploads, visible watermarks, daily manual searches, community reporting
answers.q14_would_upload_today: Yes, but only after confirming it searches Asian platforms. If it only searches Western sites, it's useless for me.
answers.q15_trust_factors: Multilingual support, Asian platform coverage, artist testimonials from anime community, mobile app availability
answers.q16_ai_analyzing_feeling: Worried. AI analyzing my art could contribute to the same AI models that are stealing from me. I need guarantees that my data isn't used for training.
answers.q17_recommend_to_others: I'd say 'It monitors Pixiv and Chinese sites for stolen anime art.' That's the selling point for my community.
answers.q18_must_do: Search Asian platforms (Pixiv, Weibo, Bilibili) and enforce takedowns in multiple languages.

answers.q1_first_reaction: I'd use it immediately. I design icons and UI illustrations that get copied into apps and websites constantly. The vault is great for version control — I often iterate on designs and need to prove which version came first. The 12 million source scan would catch unauthorized use in apps.
answers.q2_auto_action: I'd want it to generate cease-and-desist letters for commercial infringement, not just DMCA. When a startup copies my icon set into their app, that's commercial use and needs a stronger legal response.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Yes, but for vector files. My work is primarily vector — SVG, AI, EPS. Does the watermark work on vector formats? If it only works on raster images, it's useless for me.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Moderately important. Design trends move fast — if I create an icon style and someone copies it, proving timeline matters for disputes.
answers.q5_c2pa_certificate: Yes. When I license icon sets to companies, C2PA metadata adds authenticity. It's professional and adds value.
answers.q6_whats_missing: Vector file support is the biggest gap. Most tools only handle raster images. I need SVG/AI/EPS support. Also, app store monitoring — checking if stolen icons appear in iOS/Android apps.
answers.q7_one_feature: App store icon monitoring. Scan App Store and Play Store for icons that match my designs.
answers.q8_daily_usage: If it monitored Dribbble, Figma Community, and app stores for stolen icons, I'd check daily. Design theft happens on these platforms specifically.
answers.q9_unhandled_protection: AI icon generation. Tools like Midjourney can generate icon sets that are clearly derivative of popular designers' styles. No tool detects this.
answers.q10_how_often_check: Weekly. I search for my most popular icon sets on Google and occasionally check design aggregation sites.
answers.q11_platforms_shared: Dribbble (portfolio), Behance (case studies), LinkedIn (professional), Figma Community (free resources), personal website
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 30-50 icons/illustrations, plus complete icon sets
answers.q13_current_protection: Low-res previews, watermarked versions, manual searches, licensing agreements with clients
answers.q14_would_upload_today: Yes, if it supports vector files. I'd upload my entire icon library immediately.
answers.q15_trust_factors: Vector file support, design community endorsements, clear licensing terms, integration with Figma
answers.q16_ai_analyzing_feeling: Positive. AI analyzing icons for similarity is exactly what's needed. The design community needs tools to detect copying.
answers.q17_recommend_to_others: I'd say 'It found 30 unauthorized uses of my icon set across apps and websites.' Designers need this.
answers.q18_must_do: Support vector file formats and detect icon theft in app stores.

answers.q1_first_reaction: I'd be very interested but cautious. My art celebrates Black culture and identity — it gets stolen and used by brands without credit or compensation. The vault is great for keeping originals safe. The search functionality would help me find unauthorized commercial use. But I'd want to know: does it understand cultural context? Some theft is subtle — using my imagery in campaigns that don't represent the community.
answers.q2_auto_action: I'd want it to detect commercial use specifically — brands, merchandise, advertising. Also, I'd want it to generate not just DMCA but also licensing offers. Sometimes I want to negotiate fair compensation instead of just removal.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Yes, but I need it to work on fabric and textile prints. My art appears on clothing and home goods. If the watermark doesn't survive printing on fabric, it's limited.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Very important. Black artists have a long history of having work stolen without credit. Blockchain proof of creation date is powerful for establishing ownership.
answers.q5_c2pa_certificate: Yes. When licensing to brands, origin verification protects my reputation and ensures proper attribution.
answers.q6_whats_missing: Cultural context theft detection. When a brand uses Black art aesthetics without hiring Black artists, that's cultural appropriation. No tool detects this. Also, print-on-demand monitoring on platforms like Printful and Printify.
answers.q7_one_feature: Licensing negotiation integration. Instead of just takedowns, let me send licensing offers with fair pricing. Many infringers would pay if given the option.
answers.q8_daily_usage: If it monitored Etsy, Redbubble, and Amazon for unauthorized merchandise, I'd check daily. These platforms are where my art gets商业化 stolen most.
answers.q9_unhandled_protection: AI-generated art in 'Black art' style. AI models are being trained on Black artists' work to generate similar aesthetics without compensation.
answers.q10_how_often_check: 3-4 times per week. I search for my art on Etsy, Amazon, and social media.
answers.q11_platforms_shared: Instagram (main), Etsy (prints), Twitter/X (community), personal website (commissions)
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 8-12 pieces, plus prints and merchandise designs
answers.q13_current_protection: Visible watermarks, low-res uploads, manual searches, DMCA when I find theft
answers.q14_would_upload_today: Yes, but I'd want cultural context features. If it only does image matching without understanding context, it misses subtle theft.
answers.q15_trust_factors: Black artist endorsements, cultural context understanding, fair licensing options, transparent data practices
answers.q16_ai_analyzing_feeling: Cautious. AI analyzing Black art raises concerns about cultural appropriation. I need to know the AI won't learn from my work to generate similar aesthetics.
answers.q17_recommend_to_others: I'd say 'It found 20 unauthorized products using my Black art on Etsy and Amazon.' Black artists deal with this constantly.
answers.q18_must_do: Detect merchandise theft and enable fair licensing negotiations.

answers.q1_first_reaction: I'd be very interested. I create comic art and it gets stolen for web novels, fan translations, and merchandise. The vault with version history is perfect — I can prove my creation timeline for each panel. The 12 million source scan would catch unauthorized translations and reposts.
answers.q2_auto_action: I'd want it to track unauthorized translations. My comics get translated into Chinese, Korean, and other languages without permission. I'd want detection across languages and takedown in each language.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Yes, but it needs to survive panel cropping. Thieves often crop individual panels and repost them. The watermark needs to persist even when panels are separated.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Very important. In the comic industry, publication order matters. Blockchain proof of when each page was created is essential for disputes.
answers.q5_c2pa_certificate: Yes. When licensing to publishers, C2PA metadata proves authenticity and origin.
answers.q6_whats_missing: Translation detection and multilingual takedown support. Also, web novel platform monitoring — sites like Wuxiaworld and NovelUpdates host stolen comic content.
answers.q7_one_feature: Multilingual theft detection. Scan for my art in any language, any script. Chinese, Korean, Japanese, Arabic — theft doesn't respect language barriers.
answers.q8_daily_usage: If it monitored web novel platforms and translation sites, I'd check daily. These platforms move fast and content spreads quickly.
answers.q9_unhandled_protection: AI-generated web novels with stolen art. Someone takes my comic panels, uses AI to generate text, and publishes as a 'web novel.' No tool detects this hybrid theft.
answers.q10_how_often_check: Weekly. I search for my comic titles and character names across platforms.
answers.q11_platforms_shared: Instagram (portfolio), Twitter/X (community), Webtoon (publication), personal website
